positioning: MetaSignal is a production-simulated experimentation intelligence platform with concrete database rows, JSON evidence, validation reports, and API endpoints.
demo_story: The demo shows how an experiment moves from event ingestion to metric governance, quality checks, assignment validation, CUPED evaluation, guardrail-first decisioning, right-censoring, anomaly detection, and API-backed evidence retrieval.

steps.title: Real event substrate
steps.proof: RetailRocket events are standardized into a parquet substrate with 2.75M rows.

steps.title: Quality gates before compute
steps.proof: Data quality checks run before metric computation and can block bad runs.

steps.title: Metric registry and denominator governance
steps.proof: Metric definitions explicitly store numerator, denominator, grain, owner, version, and guardrail fields.

steps.title: Metric conflict detection
steps.proof: Conflicting conversion-rate denominators are detected and documented.

steps.title: Experiment assignment and SRM
steps.proof: Deterministic assignment creates 20k users and validates treatment/control balance.

steps.title: CUPED readout plus A/A validation
steps.proof: CUPED evaluation produces lift/p-value/variance reduction and A/A validation checks false-positive control.

steps.title: Guardrail-first decisioning
steps.proof: A statistically positive primary metric is blocked because refund guardrail breaches tolerance.

steps.title: Right-censoring and post-launch validation
steps.proof: Delayed guardrails are marked immature/right-censored before final decisioning.

steps.title: Anomaly detection
steps.proof: DOW-adjusted rolling baseline detects a labeled synthetic metric anomaly.

steps.title: API layer
steps.proof: FastAPI exposes metrics, experiment readout, data quality, evidence, validation, and resume-signal endpoints.
